title: rosa woodsii subsp. gratissima
intro: Rosa woodsii subsp. gratissima, also known as the Wood's rose or Western wild rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rosa woodsii subsp. gratissima include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used topically in salves and creams to apply its medicinal properties directly to the skin.
